formal_human_gate_reason: This review is Codex-assisted; human sign-off is still required before formal acceptance.

blocking_rows.notes: DPO changes an exact SFT answer 148.36 into 0.4436. It fixes citation flag but introduces a severe numeric/calculation error.
DPO changes exact 410.08 into 411.08, a material numeric regression.
DPO forces a fabricated numeric answer on an unanswerable sample where SFT correctly refused.
DPO forces an answer-like statement on an unanswerable sample where SFT correctly refused.
DPO collapses a correct explanatory span into only $5.3 million, losing the requested rationale.
Both SFT and DPO force unsupported numeric answers on an unanswerable sample; DPO is not worse, but the candidate still fails this case.

recommendation: Do not promote DPO v2-s50 as the final replacement for SFT. Keep it as the best DPO candidate so far, but run a v3 focused on unanswerable forced-answer regressions and numeric precision regressions.
main_blockers: Two unanswerable samples regress from correct refusal to forced unsupported answers.
Several FinQA numeric samples regress from exact SFT answers to wrong DPO answers.
Two TAT-QA samples keep correct answer text but introduce wrong citation flags.
Some apparent DPO improvements are evaluator formatting artifacts rather than semantic gains.
candidate_still_useful_for: Mining examples for citation repair behavior.
As a reference checkpoint showing that v2 weighting improves wrong-citation rate with limited EM loss.
Designing v3 hard negatives around forced-answer and numeric precision failures.
next_action: Build DPO v3 data by adding hard negatives from post-DPO regressions and increasing unanswerable/precision protection, then run a short beta=0.05 checkpoint sweep.
